Text and Typography

Heading Levels

Heading 1

Heading 2

Heading 3

Heading 4

Heading 5
Heading 6

Inline Text Formatting

Bold text using the strong element.

Italic text using the em element.

Bold text using the b element.

Italic text using the i element.

Underlined text using the u element.

Strikethrough text using the s element.

Deleted text and inserted text.

Highlighted text using the mark element.

Small text using the small element.

Text with subscript and superscript.

Code and Technical Text

Inline code element for short snippets.

Keyboard input: Press Ctrl + C to copy.

Sample output: Error: file not found

Variable: x = y + 1

Abbreviations and Definitions

The HTML specification is maintained by the W3C.

Semantic HTML refers to using HTML elements that convey meaning about the content they contain.

Quotations

She said, This is an inline quotation.

This is a block-level quotation. It typically spans multiple lines and is visually indented from the surrounding text.

— Example Author

Links

Standard link

Link opening in new tab

Email link

Anchor link to top

Paragraphs and Whitespace

This is the first paragraph. It contains multiple sentences to demonstrate text flow and line wrapping behavior within a constrained width container.

This is the second paragraph, separated from the first by default paragraph margins.

Line one.
Line two after a line break.
Line three after another line break.

Bidirectional Text

Left-to-right text with right-to-left override inline.

The word مرحبا is Arabic for "hello".
